description: Three major coal mines in Buchanan and Tazewell counties have gone idle after cyber damage disabled ventilation, drainage pumps, and safety monitoring systems. Over 2,000 miners are suddenly without income — most have no savings to cover even two weeks. Coal ash containment ponds at two sites are running on manual monitoring with skeleton crews; one sensor failure could trigger an uncontrolled release into the Clinch River. The coalfield counties that depend on mining severance taxes face an immediate budget crisis — school funding, road maintenance, and emergency services are all at risk. Mine operators say they cannot restart without full safety system restoration, which requires both power and cyber-secure control networks.
visible_to: AEP Appalachian Power;Governor's SW Virginia Liaison;Montgomery County Government;SW Virginia Sheriff's Coalition;VA DEQ SW Regional Office

description: Electronic prescription verification systems remain down across the region. Carilion's three dialysis centers report supply levels critical — enough for 36 hours of treatment for 200+ patients. Opioid recovery patients at four medication-assisted treatment clinics have missed two consecutive daily doses; counselors report patients returning to street sources. Rural pharmacies in Lee, Scott, and Wise counties have closed entirely — no way to verify insurance, check drug interactions, or process prescriptions. Insulin-dependent diabetics, cardiac patients on blood thinners, and organ transplant recipients on immunosuppressants face life-threatening gaps. The nearest functioning pharmacy for some rural residents is 90 minutes away.
visible_to: Carilion Clinic System;Food City / Regional Supply Chain;United Way SW Virginia;Appalachian Faith & Community Network

description: Every gas station in a 50-mile radius of Blacksburg has run dry. School districts have suspended bus service affecting 15,000 students. Three volunteer fire departments report they cannot respond to calls — tanks empty. Fuel hoarding has created dangerous situations: a garage fire in Radford was traced to 200 gallons of gasoline stored in plastic containers. Emergency vehicles regionwide are on reserve fuel. The closest fuel terminal with supply is in Roanoke, but tanker trucks need escort through areas where desperate residents have attempted to hijack deliveries.
visible_to: VDOT SW Region;AEP Appalachian Power;SW Virginia Sheriff's Coalition;SW Virginia EMA;VA DEQ SW Regional Office

description: Over 200 businesses across Blacksburg, Christiansburg, and Radford cannot process card payments. Restaurants, retail shops, and service businesses that are 95% card-based have locked their doors. Five thousand workers missed Friday's payroll — direct deposit failed and no one can cut manual checks without network access. Downtown Blacksburg is a ghost town. The cash-only economy has created lines at the few open businesses; altercations are reported at a gas station accepting cash. Three strip malls have been broken into overnight. Business owners are talking about permanent closure and leaving the region.
visible_to: Montgomery County Government;Shentel Communications;WDBJ7 News;CISA Regional Coordinator

description: Food City's regional warehouse in Salem is operating on backup generators with 48 hours of fuel. Refrigeration is failing — $2M in perishable inventory will spoil within 24 hours if power isn't restored. Thirty-seven rural Food City and Dollar General stores have empty shelves. The panic buying that began in Phase 2 depleted reserves that were never restocked. Church food pantries report they've distributed everything they had. Families in Appalachian communities 30+ miles from the nearest town have no food access at all. A semi-organized caravan of residents from three counties is heading to the Salem warehouse demanding access.
visible_to: Food City / Regional Supply Chain;SW Virginia EMA;Appalachian Faith & Community Network;United Way SW Virginia;SW Virginia Sheriff's Coalition

description: Virginia Tech's registrar reports 340 students have submitted mid-semester transfer requests — the largest wave in university history. Young families are listing homes in Blacksburg and Christiansburg at steep discounts. Carilion has lost 12 nurses and 3 physicians to out-of-state offers this week alone. The viral editorial 'Is Southwest Virginia Dying?' has been shared 100,000 times. A Roanoke Times poll shows 40% of residents under 35 are 'seriously considering' leaving. The Chamber of Commerce warns that if the perception of collapse isn't countered, the economic damage will become self-fulfilling regardless of whether systems are restored.
visible_to: Virginia Tech Crisis Response;Governor's SW Virginia Liaison;WDBJ7 News;Carilion Clinic System

description: FEMA's Region III office has formally ruled that the cyber attack does not meet the Stafford Act definition of a 'major disaster' or 'emergency' because there is no physical damage to infrastructure. This freezes $50M in federal aid that Virginia's governor requested. The congressional delegation is split — one senator supports emergency legislation while the other calls it 'federal overreach.' Without federal funds, the state and local governments must cover all response costs from existing budgets. Legal experts say a challenge could take months. Meanwhile, neighboring West Virginia counties affected by the same attack have found a workaround through a different federal program.
visible_to: CISA Regional Coordinator;Governor's SW Virginia Liaison;VA DEQ SW Regional Office;Virginia Tech Crisis Response

description: The banking network serving Southwest Virginia remains offline. ATMs are non-functional. The three regional banks have closed branches — they cannot process any transactions. Food City registers are cash-only but running low on change. VDOT toll systems on I-81 are down, creating traffic backups. Montgomery County has suspended tax and utility bill collection — creating a local government revenue crisis. Unemployment claims can't be filed online. Social Security direct deposits aren't clearing. The elderly and disabled who depend on electronic benefit transfers have no access to their own money.
visible_to: Shentel Communications;Montgomery County Government;VDOT SW Region;CISA Regional Coordinator
